config.detail: Hydra-style config under cfg.model.cfg. Encoder + frontend match the gigaam-v3 base branches (16-layer Conformer, d_model=768, rotary attn, conv1d subsampling). head=CTCHead with feat_in=768, num_classes=71 (70 chars + 1 blank). decoding=CTCGreedyDecoding with INLINE 70-entry multilingual character vocabulary (no tokenizer.model file). model_name=multilingual_ctc; model_class=ctc. NOTE: the `main` branch is identical to the `ctc` branch (both model_name=multilingual_ctc); `large_ctc` is a separate 24-layer/d_model=1024 sibling.

preprocessor.detail: Frontend declared inside config.json under cfg.model.cfg.preprocessor: _target_=modeling_gigaam.FeatureExtractor, features=64, n_fft=320, hop=160, win=320, center=false. Same FeatureExtractor class and args as the gigaam-v3 branches; mel_scale=htk, mel_norm=null, SpecScaler log scaling come from the FeatureExtractor defaults. Stage 2/3 must confirm modeling_gigaam.py frontend is byte-identical to v3.

tokenizer_config.detail: Charwise tokenizer; vocabulary is inline in config.json under cfg.model.cfg.decoding.vocabulary (70 strings). Multilingual: space + apostrophe + Latin a-z (26) + Cyrillic а-я+ё (33) + 9 Turkic/Kazakh letters (і ғ қ ң ү ұ һ ә ө). NO tokenizer.model file (Tokenizer.charwise=True path, same as gigaam-v3-ctc/rnnt).

tokenizer_model.detail: Branch has NO tokenizer.model file — charwise path. Vocab is the inline 70-entry list.

generation_config.detail: Decoding is CTC greedy; no HF generation_config.

model_weights.detail: PyTorch pickle, 883,170,115 bytes (~883 MB) on the main/ctc branch. ~220M params at F32, matching the '220M' model-card figure. large_ctc's pytorch_model.bin is 2,341,592,643 bytes (~2.34 GB, ~600M params).

safetensors_metadata.detail: Repo ships PyTorch pickle, not safetensors.

model_card.detail: Single model card covers all four multilingual branches (ssl, ctc, large_ssl, large_ctc). Reports CV/FLEURS/Internal WER for ru/en/kk/ky/uz. arXiv:2607.10371. Trained HuBERT-style on 2M hours / 70+ languages, fine-tuned for ASR with charwise CTC on 50K hours.

reference_modeling_code.detail: Author-first PyTorch implementation. License MIT. Same ConformerEncoder/CTCHead/FeatureExtractor classes as gigaam-v3. README documents the reference path as transformers.AutoModel.from_pretrained('ai-sage/GigaAM-Multilingual', revision='ctc', trust_remote_code=True).transcribe(wav). Stage 2 must confirm whether the gigaam PyPI package also exposes a load_model name for this checkpoint.

key_fields.tokenizer.vocab_inline: 70 entries: space + apostrophe + Latin a-z (26) + Cyrillic а-я+ё (33) + 9 Turkic/Kazakh letters (і ғ қ ң ү ұ һ ә ө). Blank id = 70.

dtype.evidence: pytorch_model.bin is 883 MB ≈ 220M params at F32. salute-developers/GigaAM PyTorch checkpoints are stored at F32 (same as the gigaam-v3 family).

upstream_benchmarks.notes: GigaAM Multilingual (220M ctc) column. Normalization: lowercasing, punctuation removal, numerals→words; utterances >30s and digit-containing refs excluded; greedy decoding.
GigaAM Multilingual (220M ctc) column. English is explicitly 'moderate quality' per the model card.
GigaAM Multilingual (220M ctc) column. English is explicitly 'moderate quality' per the model card.

reference_rationale: Same canonical reference as the rest of the gigaam family: salute-developers/GigaAM (MIT). The multilingual README documents the reference inference path as transformers.AutoModel.from_pretrained('ai-sage/GigaAM-Multilingual', revision='ctc', trust_remote_code=True).transcribe(wav), which runs the same modeling_gigaam.py classes the gigaam-v3 port already validated against. Stage 2 pins the exact loader entrypoint (AutoModel trust_remote_code vs a gigaam.load_model name).
known_risks: Multilingual with NO language conditioning: the model is a single Conformer + CTC head over one shared 70-char vocab. There are no language tokens, no language embeddings, and no language hint input. A --language flag is a no-op for this model — it decodes characters directly. The capability table must not claim an 'explicit language hint' observable the model cannot honor.

70-entry charwise vocab spans THREE scripts: Latin a-z, Cyrillic (Russian а-я + ё), and 9 Turkic/Kazakh Cyrillic letters (і ғ қ ң ү ұ һ ә ө). Apostrophe (') is in-vocab (Uzbek Latin oʻ/gʻ and English contractions). Unlike gigaam-v3-ctc, ё IS present. NO digits, NO punctuation beyond apostrophe, NO casing — output is lowercased. Converter must emit the exact 70-entry list; C++ loader reuses the charwise path but with a wider vocab.
Blank id = 70 = num_classes - 1 (num_classes=71). CTCHead is nn.Conv1d(768, 71, kernel_size=1) + log_softmax(-1). Same head shape as gigaam-v3-ctc but wider output.

Encoder + frontend are EXPECTED identical to the gigaam-v3 base branches (16-layer Conformer, d=768, rotary PE, conv1d subsampling, htk mel, center=false, SpecScaler log). This repo's modeling_gigaam.py is a NEWER regenerated build (2149 lines vs v3's 1426, sha differs) that adds word-timestamp + longform machinery (Word/frames_to_words/LongformTranscriptionResult/masked flash-attn), but the core math classes (FeatureExtractor, SpecScaler, ConformerEncoder, RotaryPositionMultiHeadAttention, apply_rotary_pos_emb, rtt_half, CTCHead, CTCGreedyDecoding) are all present and structurally the same. Stage 2 must dump the frontend/encoder and confirm bit-exactness against the v3 math; do not assume weight reuse (v3 Stage 2 found per-variant encoder-weight divergence).
Rotary positional embeddings inside Conformer attention — same [cos;sin] stacked-PE / rtt_half element-ordering risk as the gigaam-v3 variants.

Reference entrypoint is documented as HF AutoModel trust_remote_code (revision='ctc'), NOT a named gigaam.load_model() call in this README. The gigaam PyPI package may not ship a multilingual model name; Stage 2 must confirm the reference loader and whether scripts/envs/gigaam needs a transformers>=5 / torch 2.10 bump (README recommends transformers==5.*, torch==2.10.*).

Acceptance is FLEURS, gated on ru + kk to start (user-signed 2026-07-18), expanding to ky/uz/en once the C++ port is trusted — not LibriSpeech. Per-language text normalization differs (Kazakh/Kyrgyz/Uzbek Cyrillic orthography, Uzbek apostrophes, English casing/punctuation). The WER scorer must apply the model-card normalization per language: lowercase, strip punctuation, numerals→words, and match the model's lowercased charwise output. Getting normalization wrong will inflate WER differently per language. Kazakh (kk) exercises the 9 Turkic vocab letters that Russian does not, so it is the key early-signal language alongside ru.
English is 'moderate quality' (CV 26.0%, FLEURS 12.2%) — LibriSpeech test-clean would test the model's weakest capability and is NOT the acceptance dataset for this multilingual model.

large_ctc is a DIFFERENT architecture (24 layers, d_model=1024, feat_in=1024) sharing the same 70-char vocab and CTC head width — it needs its own intake/oracle/convert run, not a tolerance reuse from the 220M ctc.
Long-form (>30s) path: same PyAnnote VAD dependency as gigaam-v3 — out of scope for the C++ port at parity. Model-card benchmarks explicitly exclude utterances >30s.

intake_gaps.reason: RESOLVED at Stage 2: sha256 over the newline-joined inline 70-entry charwise vocab = c74f14b62f397a12ab3d1d2db0986ae1ae929f11a65465bd8ea2db06e8022f49. Recorded in tokenizer.vocab_sha256 and the golden manifest tokenizer_summary._vocab_sha256. Expected to MATCH the large_ctc branch vocab and DIFFER from every gigaam-v3 vocab; large_ctc intake will confirm the match.
Filled at Stage 4 once we observe which norm/bias tensors must stay F32 under quant.

RESOLVED at Stage 2: reference loads via gigaam.load_model('multilingual_ctc', fp16_encoder=False, device='cpu') from the author package (the gigaam PyPI HEAD registers the multilingual checkpoints; the README's AutoModel trust_remote_code path was avoided because its modeling_gigaam.py check_imports demands pyannote for out-of-scope longform). Runs under a DEDICATED env scripts/envs/gigaam-multilingual (gigaam @559d88d, torch/torchaudio 2.11 pinned) so the shipped v3 env stays at 6e4b027. Dumper: scripts/dump_reference_gigaam_author.py --variant multilingual_ctc. Verified transcript on samples/ru.wav: 'важно различать глаголы и дополнения'.

Model-card FLEURS numbers use upstream's own normalization and >30s/digit exclusions. Stage 7 measures the reference baseline ourselves per language over the ingested FLEURS manifests and gates C++ against that measured baseline, not the published number.
